# I-rdy-002 (#498) — Phase 1 gap verification against the live system

**Status:** verification complete (v2, post Codex iter-1). Verified against the
live orchestrator (OVH BHS5) + static code inspection of the repo.

## Verified results

| Gap | Verified status | Evidence |
|---|---|---|
| **Auth** | **CONFIRMED-BROKEN** | `web/app/sign-in/page.tsx` is a disabled placeholder — all inputs `disabled`, copy "Authentication is a placeholder for Phase 0", no form/onSubmit/login call. `web/lib/api.ts` — every `fetch()` sends only `content-type`, zero `Authorization` headers. UI cannot operate with auth on. → I-rdy-004 (#500). |
| **Rich UI fixture-bound** (F5-F15: bundle, charts, follow-up, compare) | **CONFIRMED-BROKEN** | `src/polaris_v6/api/bundle.py:45-64` — `_GOLDEN_RUN_INDEX` dict; `bundle.py:58` does `_GOLDEN_RUN_INDEX.get(run_id)` → 404 for any non-golden run. `charts.py:9,22`, `followup.py:10,24`, `compare.py:10,18` all import `_GOLDEN_RUN_INDEX` and do the same. A real completed run ID will always 404 — fixture-only. No LLM needed to confirm. → I-rdy-008 (#504). |
| **Canonical templates** | **CONFIRMED-BROKEN** | `web/app/page.tsx` template cards = `clinical, housing, climate, ai_sovereignty, canada_us, defense, trade, workforce`. Canonical 8 (`docs/polaris_locked_scope.md` §2) = `clinical, policy, tech, due_diligence, ai_sovereignty, canada_us, workforce, custom`. `housing/climate/defense/trade` are non-canonical; `policy/tech/due_diligence/custom` are missing. → I-rdy-005 (#501). |
| **Worker document_ids** | **CONFIRMED-BROKEN** | `POST /runs` sends `payload.model_dump()` to the actor so `document_ids` reaches `request_payload`, but `src/polaris_v6/queue/actors.py` builds the query object `q` for `run_one_query` WITHOUT `document_ids` — uploaded docs never reach the pipeline. → I-rdy-010 (#506). |
| Model/env alignment | **CONFIRMED — stale refs present** | stale DeepSeek V4 Flash / qwen references are repo-visible (architecture.md, hardware_decision.md, transparency defaults). Fix tracked in I-rdy-006 (#502). |
| Coherent product journey | **CONFIRMED-BROKEN** | `web/app/layout.tsx` has no global nav; 17/33 routes are harness pages; no single land→run→report→inspect journey. → I-rdy-014 (#510). |
| Run create (`POST /runs`) | **WORKS** | returns a real `run_id`, `lifecycle_status: queued`. |
| Run status (`GET /runs/{id}`) | **WORKS** with a live run ID | 200 for the real run `54f42c11…`. |
| cancel/resume | **PARTIAL** | `cancel_research_run` actor exists (`actors.py:214`) but records the request only ("real cancellation via Worker.send_signal"); no cancel endpoint on the API; resume unverified. → I-rdy-011 (#507). |
| F14 memory durability | **CONFIRMED in-memory** | `src/polaris_v6/api/memory.py:18,22` — live path uses `WorkspaceMemoryStore()` (in-memory `store.py`). `chroma_store.py` exists but is NOT the wired backend; comment says "the storage backend [will move] to Chroma". Not durable. → I-rdy-012 (#508). |
| GPG bundle signing | **PARTIAL** | `gpg_signer.py` exists; `/runs/{id}/bundle.tar.gz` appears to have live run-store/artifact-dir signing wiring; clean-machine verification not proven. → tracked in Workstream L (I-rdy-017). |

## Conclusion

Phase 1 verification is complete via the live system + static code
inspection. **Five P0 gaps are CONFIRMED-BROKEN** (auth, rich-UI fixture-bound,
canonical templates, worker document_ids, coherent journey) — all are genuine
builds, not verify-and-close. Model/env has confirmed stale refs. cancel/resume,
F14 memory, GPG signing are PARTIAL. No gap requires a completed run to verify
— the rich-UI endpoints are provably fixture-bound by code (`_GOLDEN_RUN_INDEX`).

Feeds Phase 0B (I-rdy-003, #499): the lock doc's provisional feature statuses
can now be set to these verified statuses.

codex
The updated document now contains the previously missing high-severity items, but I’m checking the precise code references rather than trusting the narrative. The first pass already shows the worktree is dirty with many unrelated files, so I’ll ignore those unless they touch this review target.

 succeeded in 2490ms:
   1: """GET /runs/{run_id}/bundle — F15 audit bundle export.
   2: 
   3: This is the artifact Carney's office receives. Per docs/blockers.md §5,
   4: real bundle redistribution policy is gated on Phase 1/2 legal IP review;
   5: for COPYRIGHTED sources the bundle exports citations + DOI links only,
   6: not verbatim spans.
   7: 
   8: Phase 0 ships the endpoint contract that returns an EvidenceContract
   9: v1.0 JSON. Phase 1 wires it to the actual run-storage backend; here we
  10: serve a synthetic bundle from the golden corpus when a known run_id
  11: matches, otherwise 404.
  12: 
  13: I-arch-001d (2026-05-13) adds GET /runs/{run_id}/bundle.tar.gz which
  14: resolves run_id → artifact_dir via run_store and returns a real
  15: GPG-signed audit bundle via the build_slice_chain bridge.
  16: """
  17: 
  18: from __future__ import annotations
  19: 
  20: import json  # noqa: F401 — used by release_allowed gate below
  21: from pathlib import Path
  22: 
  23: from fastapi import APIRouter, Depends, HTTPException
  24: 
  25: # I-arch-001d Codex diff iter-1 P1-001 fix: import get_sign_fn at module top
  26: # so FastAPI Depends() identity matches what app.dependency_overrides keys on.
  27: # A lambda Depends would be a DIFFERENT callable than get_sign_fn — the
  28: # create_app() override wouldn't fire and the endpoint would always 503.
  29: from polaris_graph.api.audit_bundle_route import (
  30:     AuditBundleRequest,
  31:     get_sign_fn,
  32:     post_audit_bundle,
  33: )
  34: from polaris_v6.api.artifact_to_slice_chain import (
  35:     SovereigntyFilterEmptiedReportError,
  36:     build_slice_chain,
  37: )
  38: from polaris_v6.queue import run_store
  39: from polaris_v6.schemas.evidence_contract import EvidenceContract
  40: 
  41: router = APIRouter(prefix="/runs", tags=["bundle"])
  42: 
  43: _FIXTURE_DIR = Path(__file__).resolve().parents[3] / "tests" / "v6" / "fixtures" / "evidence_contract_v1"
  44: 
  45: _GOLDEN_RUN_INDEX = {
  46:     "golden_clinical_001": "golden_run_clinical.json",
  47:     "golden_housing_002": "golden_run_with_contradiction.json",
  48:     "golden_abort_003": "golden_run_abort_no_verified.json",
  49:     "golden_defense_004": "golden_run_defense.json",
  50:     "golden_climate_005": "golden_run_climate.json",
  51:     "golden_ai_006": "golden_run_ai_sovereignty.json",
  52:     "golden_with_drop_reason": "golden_run_with_drop_reason.json",
  53: }
  54: 
  55: 
  56: @router.get("/{run_id}/bundle", response_model=EvidenceContract)
  57: def get_bundle(run_id: str) -> EvidenceContract:
  58:     fixture_name = _GOLDEN_RUN_INDEX.get(run_id)
  59:     if fixture_name is None:
  60:         raise HTTPException(
  61:             status_code=404,
  62:             detail=f"Bundle for run {run_id!r} not found. Available golden runs: {list(_GOLDEN_RUN_INDEX)}",
  63:         )
  64:     raw = json.loads((_FIXTURE_DIR / fixture_name).read_text())
  65:     return EvidenceContract.model_validate(raw)
  66: 
  67: 
  68: @router.get("/{run_id}/bundle.tar.gz")
  69: def get_run_bundle_targz(
  70:     run_id: str,
  71:     sign_fn=Depends(get_sign_fn),
  72: ):
  73:     """Resolve run_id → artifact_dir → signed audit bundle (tar.gz).
  74: 
  75:     I-arch-001d Codex diff iter-1 P1-001: `Depends(get_sign_fn)` MUST use
  76:     the actual `get_sign_fn` callable from audit_bundle_route — the
  77:     create_app() registers `app.dependency_overrides[get_sign_fn]` to
  78:     inject the real GPGSigner when POLARIS_GPG_KEY_ID is set. A lambda
  79:     Depends would be a different callable; the override would never fire.
  80: 
  81:     Status codes:
  82:         200: bundleable run; returns application/gzip
  83:         404: run not found, not completed, or artifact_dir missing
  84:         422: run aborted (pipeline_status=abort_*) or sovereignty cascade
  85:              emptied the report
  86:         503: GPG signer not configured (POLARIS_GPG_KEY_ID unset) — surfaced
  87:              by the inner post_audit_bundle when sign_fn is None.
  88:     """
  89:     info = run_store.get_run(run_id)
  90:     if info is None:
  91:         raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail={"error": "run not found"})
  92:     if info.lifecycle_status != "completed":
  93:         raise HTTPException(
  94:             status_code=404,
  95:             detail={"error": f"run not completed: lifecycle_status={info.lifecycle_status}"},
  96:         )
  97:     if info.pipeline_status and info.pipeline_status.startswith("abort_"):
  98:         raise HTTPException(
  99:             status_code=422,
 100:             detail={"error": f"run aborted: pipeline_status={info.pipeline_status}", "bundleable": False},
 101:         )
 102:     if not info.artifact_dir:
 103:         raise HTTPException(
 104:             status_code=404, detail={"error": "run has no artifact_dir recorded"}
 105:         )
 106: 
 107:     artifact_dir = Path(info.artifact_dir)
 108:     if not artifact_dir.is_dir():
 109:         raise HTTPException(
 110:             status_code=404,
 111:             detail={"error": f"artifact_dir does not exist on disk: {artifact_dir}"},
 112:         )
 113: 
 114:     # I-arch-001d Codex diff iter-2 P1-002 fix: release_allowed gate.
 115:     # The slice-chain pipeline_verdict collapses partial_* into "success",
 116:     # but a release-blocked partial (e.g. partial_qwen_advisory with
 117:     # release_allowed=false) MUST NOT ship as a clean bundle. Read the raw
 118:     # manifest.release_allowed flag and refuse with 422 when False.
 119:     try:
 120:         manifest_raw = json.loads((artifact_dir / "manifest.json").read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
 121:     except (OSError, json.JSONDecodeError) as exc:
 122:         raise HTTPException(
 123:             status_code=404,
 124:             detail={"error": f"manifest.json missing or invalid: {exc}"},
 125:         ) from exc
 126:     if not manifest_raw.get("release_allowed", False):
 127:         raise HTTPException(
 128:             status_code=422,
 129:             detail={
 130:                 "error": (
 131:                     f"run release-blocked: pipeline_status={info.pipeline_status!r}, "
 132:                     f"release_allowed=False. Bundle cannot ship until release gate clears."
 133:                 ),
 134:                 "bundleable": False,
 135:                 "pipeline_status": info.pipeline_status,
 136:             },
 137:         )
 138: 
 139:     try:
 140:         decision, pool, report = build_slice_chain(artifact_dir)
 141:     except SovereigntyFilterEmptiedReportError as exc:
 142:         raise HTTPException(status_code=422, detail={"error": str(exc)}) from exc
 143:     except FileNotFoundError as exc:
 144:         raise HTTPException(
 145:             status_code=404,
 146:             detail={"error": f"artifact_dir incomplete: {exc}"},
 147:         ) from exc
 148: 
 149:     return post_audit_bundle(
 150:         AuditBundleRequest(decision=decision, pool=pool, report=report),
 151:         sign_fn=sign_fn,
 152:     )

  18: 
  19:

 succeeded in 3216ms:
   1: """GET /runs/{left}/compare/{right} — F12 side-by-side compare endpoint."""
   2: 
   3: from __future__ import annotations
   4: 
   5: import json
   6: from dataclasses import asdict
   7: 
   8: from fastapi import APIRouter, HTTPException
   9: 
  10: from polaris_v6.api.bundle import _FIXTURE_DIR, _GOLDEN_RUN_INDEX
  11: from polaris_v6.compare.differ import compare_reports
  12: from polaris_v6.schemas.evidence_contract import EvidenceContract
  13: 
  14: router = APIRouter(prefix="/runs", tags=["compare"])
  15: 
  16: 
  17: def _load(run_id: str) -> EvidenceContract:
  18:     fixture_name = _GOLDEN_RUN_INDEX.get(run_id)
  19:     if fixture_name is None:
  20:         raise HTTPException(
  21:             status_code=404, detail=f"Bundle for run {run_id!r} not found."
  22:         )
  23:     raw = json.loads((_FIXTURE_DIR / fixture_name).read_text())
  24:     return EvidenceContract.model_validate(raw)
  25: 
  26: 
  27: @router.get("/{left_run_id}/compare/{right_run_id}")
  28: def get_compare(left_run_id: str, right_run_id: str) -> dict:
  29:     if left_run_id == right_run_id:
  30:         raise HTTPException(
  31:             status_code=400, detail="compare requires two distinct run ids"
  32:         )
  33:     left = _load(left_run_id)
  34:     right = _load(right_run_id)
  35:     return asdict(compare_reports(left, right))

 143:                   className={
 144:                     tpl.active
 145:                       ? "flex flex-col"
 146:                       : "bg-muted/40 text-muted-foreground flex flex-col"
 147:                   }
 148:                   data-testid={`template-card-${tpl.id}`}
 149:                 >
 150:                   <CardHeader>
 151:                     <div className="flex items-start justify-between gap-2">
 152:                       <CardTitle className="text-lg">{tpl.name}</CardTitle>
 153:                       {!tpl.active ? (
 154:                         <span
 155:                           aria-hidden="true"
 156:                           className="bg-background text-muted-foreground border-border rounded-full border px-2 py-0.5 text-[10px] font-medium tracking-widest uppercase"
 157:                         >
 158:                           Coming soon
 159:                         </span>
 160:                       ) : null}
 161:                     </div>
 162:                     <CardDescription className="text-sm">
 163:                       {tpl.summary}
 164:                     </CardDescription>
 165:                   </CardHeader>
 166:                   <CardContent className="flex flex-1 flex-col justify-between gap-4">
 167:                     <div className="flex flex-col gap-2 text-sm">
 168:                       <p>
 169:                         <span className="text-foreground font-medium">
 170:                           In scope:
 171:                         </span>{" "}
 172:                         {tpl.sample_question}
 173:                       </p>
 174:                       <p>
 175:                         <span className="text-foreground font-medium">
 176:                           Out of scope:
 177:                         </span>{" "}
 178:                         {tpl.out_of_scope}
 179:                       </p>
 180:                     </div>
 181:                     {tpl.active ? (
 182:                       <Button
 183:                         variant="outline"
 184:                         nativeButton={false}
 185:                         render={
 186:                           <Link
 187:                             data-testid={`template-card-${tpl.id}-link`}
 188:                             href={`/intake?template=${tpl.id}`}
 189:                           />
 190:                         }
 191:                       >
 192:                         Open {tpl.name}
 193:                       </Button>
 194:                     ) : (
 195:                       <Button
 196:                         variant="outline"
 197:                         disabled
 198:                         tabIndex={-1}
 199:                         aria-disabled="true"
 200:                       >
 201:                         Coming soon
 202:                       </Button>
 203:                     )}
 204:                   </CardContent>
 205:                 </Card>
 206:               ))}
 207:             </div>
 208:           </section>
 209:         </main>
 210: 
 211:         <footer className="border-border bg-background border-t">
 212:           <div className="text-muted-foreground mx-auto flex w-full max-w-6xl items-center justify-between px-6 py-4 text-xs">
 213:             <span>POLARIS v6.2 — slices 1-5 shipped</span>
 214:             <span>Sovereign Canadian deep research</span>
 215:           </div>
 216:         </footer>
 217:       </HomeKeyboardShell>
 218:     </div>
 219:   );
 220: }

 succeeded in 3554ms:
   1: /**
   2:  * POLARIS v6 API client.
   3:  *
   4:  * Mirrors the FastAPI contract in src/polaris_v6/api/{runs,health,stream}.py.
   5:  *
   6:  * I-carney-005 (2026-05-13): all requests are browser-relative paths so the
   7:  * browser only ever talks to the webui origin. Next.js server-side rewrites
   8:  * (web/next.config.ts) forward `/api/v6/*` → `${INTERNAL_API_URL}/*`, which
   9:  * in production compose is `http://api:8000` (a Docker service name the
  10:  * browser cannot resolve directly). In local dev, INTERNAL_API_URL defaults
  11:  * to http://localhost:8000 so `npm run dev` against a host-port-mapped
  12:  * backend works without any client-side configuration.
  13:  *
  14:  * Codex diff iter-1 P1-B: NEXT_PUBLIC_BACKEND_URL is no longer consulted at
  15:  * runtime — baking an absolute http://api:8000 into the client bundle would
  16:  * make production browsers attempt direct Docker-network reach and fail.
  17:  */
  18: 
  19: const BACKEND_URL = "/api/v6";

  48: export interface RunStatusResponse {
  49:   run_id: string;
  50:   status: RunStatus;

  58: export interface ApiError extends Error {
  59:   status: number;
  60:   body: unknown;
  61: }
  62: 
  63: async function asJsonOrThrow<T>(response: Response): Promise<T> {
  64:   const body = await response.json().catch(() => null);
  65:   if (!response.ok) {
  66:     const error = new Error(
  67:       `POLARIS backend returned ${response.status}`,
  68:     ) as ApiError;
  69:     error.status = response.status;
  70:     error.body = body;
  71:     throw error;
  72:   }
  73:   return body as T;
  74: }
  75: 
  76: export async function createRun(
  77:   payload: RunRequest,
  78: ): Promise<RunStatusResponse> {
  79:   const response = await fetch(`${BACKEND_URL}/runs`, {
  80:     method: "POST",
  81:     headers: { "content-type": "application/json" },
  82:     body: JSON.stringify(payload),
  83:   });
  84:   return asJsonOrThrow<RunStatusResponse>(response);
  85: }
  86: 
  87: export async function getRun(runId: string): Promise<RunStatusResponse> {
  88:   const response = await fetch(`${BACKEND_URL}/runs/${runId}`);
  89:   return asJsonOrThrow<RunStatusResponse>(response);
  90: }

 124:   form.append("classification", classification);

 294: export function downloadBundleAsJson(bundle: EvidenceContract): void {
 295:   const blob = new Blob([JSON.stringify(bundle, null, 2)], {
 296:     type: "application/json",
 297:   });
 298:   const url = URL.createObjectURL(blob);
 299:   const link = document.createElement("a");
 300:   link.href = url;
 301:   link.download = `polaris_bundle_${bundle.run_id}.json`;
 302:   document.body.appendChild(link);
 303:   link.click();
 304:   document.body.removeChild(link);
 305:   URL.revokeObjectURL(url);
 306: }
 307: 
 308: export interface StreamEvent {
 309:   event: string;
 310:   data: Record<string, unknown>;
 311: }
 312: 
 313: /**
 314:  * Subscribe to /stream/{runId}. Returns the EventSource so the caller can
 315:  * close it. Per Phase 0 stub, the backend yields 5 deterministic events.
 316:  */
 317: export function subscribeToRun(
 318:   runId: string,
 319:   onEvent: (event: StreamEvent) => void,
 320:   onError?: (error: Event) => void,
 321: ): EventSource {
 322:   const source = new EventSource(`${BACKEND_URL}/stream/${runId}`);
 323: 
 324:   const handler = (event: MessageEvent<string>) => {
 325:     try {
 326:       const parsed = JSON.parse(event.data);
 327:       onEvent({ event: event.type, data: parsed });
 328:     } catch {
 329:       onEvent({ event: event.type, data: { raw: event.data } });
 330:     }
 331:   };
 332: 
 333:   for (const eventName of [
 334:     "scope_decision",
 335:     "retrieval_progress",
 336:     "verifier_verdict",
 337:     "section_complete",
 338:     "run_complete",
 339:   ]) {
 340:     source.addEventListener(eventName, handler);
 341:   }
 342: 
 343:   if (onError) {
 344:     source.onerror = onError;
 345:   }
 346:   return source;
 347: }

 412: export interface IntakeSuccessResponse {

 418: export interface IntakeErrorBody {
 419:   error: true;
 420:   code: "too_short" | "too_long" | "invalid_input";
 421:   message: string;
 422:   raw: string;
 423: }
 424: 
 425: export class IntakeBadRequestError extends Error {
 426:   code: IntakeErrorBody["code"];
 427:   raw: string;
 428:   constructor(body: IntakeErrorBody) {
 429:     super(body.message);
 430:     this.name = "IntakeBadRequestError";
 431:     this.code = body.code;
 432:     this.raw = body.raw;
 433:   }
 434: }
 435: 
 436: export async function runIntake(
 437:   question: string,
 438: ): Promise<IntakeSuccessResponse> {
 439:   const response = await fetch(`${BACKEND_URL}/api/intake`, {
 440:     method: "POST",
 441:     headers: { "content-type": "application/json" },
 442:     body: JSON.stringify({ question }),
 443:   });
 444: 
 445:   if (response.status === 400) {
 446:     const detail = await response.json().catch(() => null);
 447:     const body = (detail?.detail ?? detail) as IntakeErrorBody | null;
 448:     if (body && body.error) {
 449:       throw new IntakeBadRequestError(body);
 450:     }
 451:   }
 452: 
 453:   return asJsonOrThrow<IntakeSuccessResponse>(response);
 454: }

 473: export type RetrievalSourceTier = "T1" | "T2" | "T3";
 474: 
 475: export interface RetrievalSource {
 476:   source_id: string;
 477:   url: string;
 478:   domain: string;
 479:   tier: RetrievalSourceTier;
 480:   title: string;
 481:   publication_date: string | null;
 482:   authors: string[];
 483:   snippet: string;
 484:   full_text_available: boolean;
 485:   full_text: string | null;
 486:   fetched_at_utc: string;
 487:   provenance: Record<string, unknown>;
 488:   retracted?: boolean;
 489: }
 490: 
 491: export interface RetrievalAdequacyVerdict {
 492:   is_adequate: boolean;
 493:   sources_per_tier: Record<RetrievalSourceTier, number>;
 494:   min_required_per_tier: Record<RetrievalSourceTier, number>;
 495:   failure_reason: string | null;
 496: }
 497: 
 498: export interface EvidencePool {
 499:   pool_id: string;
 500:   decision_id: string;
 501:   sources: RetrievalSource[];
 502:   adequacy: RetrievalAdequacyVerdict;
 503:   queries_executed: string[];
 504:   retrieval_started_at_utc: string;
 505:   retrieval_finished_at_utc: string;
 506:   latency_ms: number;
 507:   cost_usd: number;
 508: }

 511:   error: false;
 512:   pool: EvidencePool;
 513:   server_time_utc: string;
 514: }
 515: 
 516: export interface RetrievalErrorBody {
 517:   error: true;
 518:   code: "wrong_status" | "wrong_scope_class" | "fetch_backend_unavailable";
 519:   message: string;
 520:   decision_id: string | null;
 521: }
 522: 
 523: export class RetrievalBadRequestError extends Error {
 524:   code: RetrievalErrorBody["code"];
 525:   decision_id: string | null;
 526:   constructor(body: RetrievalErrorBody) {
 527:     super(body.message);
 528:     this.name = "RetrievalBadRequestError";
 529:     this.code = body.code;
 530:     this.decision_id = body.decision_id;
 531:   }
 532: }
 533: 
 534: export async function runRetrieval(
 535:   decision: IntakeScopeDecision,
 536: ): Promise<RetrievalSuccessResponse> {
 537:   const response = await fetch(`${BACKEND_URL}/api/retrieval`, {
 538:     method: "POST",
 539:     headers: { "content-type": "application/json" },
 540:     body: JSON.stringify({ decision }),
 541:   });
 542: 
 543:   if (response.status === 400) {
 544:     const detail = await response.json().catch(() => null);
 545:     const body = (detail?.detail ?? detail) as RetrievalErrorBody | null;
 546:     if (body && body.error) {
 547:       throw new RetrievalBadRequestError(body);
 548:     }
 549:   }
 550: 
 551:   return asJsonOrThrow<RetrievalSuccessResponse>(response);
 552: }
 553: 
 554: export interface RetrievalHealthResponse {
 555:   status: "ok";
 556:   slice: string;
 557:   pipeline_stages: string[];
 558:   fetch_backend: string;
 559: }
 560: 
 561: export async function getRetrievalHealth(): Promise<RetrievalHealthResponse> {
 562:   const response = await fetch(`${BACKEND_URL}/api/retrieval/health`);
 563:   return asJsonOrThrow<RetrievalHealthResponse>(response);
 564: }
 565: 
 566: /** Convenience: count sources matching a given tier. */
 567: export function countSourcesByTier(
 568:   pool: EvidencePool,
 569:   tier: RetrievalSourceTier,
 570: ): number {
 571:   return pool.sources.filter((s) => s.tier === tier).length;
 572: }
 573: 
 574: /** Convenience: dedupe + sort sources by tier (T1 first), then by domain. */
 575: export function sortSourcesByTier(
 576:   sources: RetrievalSource[],
 577: ): RetrievalSource[] {
 578:   const tier_rank: Record<RetrievalSourceTier, number> = {
 579:     T1: 0,
 580:     T2: 1,
 581:     T3: 2,
 582:   };
 583:   return [...sources].sort((a, b) => {
 584:     const t = tier_rank[a.tier] - tier_rank[b.tier];
 585:     if (t !== 0) return t;
 586:     return a.domain.localeCompare(b.domain);
 587:   });
 588: }

 606: 
 607: export interface ReportVerifiedSentence {
 608:   section_id: string;
 609:   sentence_text: string;
 610:   provenance_tokens: string[];

 735: }
 736: 
 737: export interface GenerationErrorBody {
 738:   error: true;
 739:   code:
 740:     | "inadequate_pool"
 741:     | "completion_backend_unavailable"
 742:     | "malformed_output";
 743:   message: string;
 744:   pool_id: string | null;
 745:   decision_id: string | null;
 746: }
 747: 
 748: export class GenerationBadRequestError extends Error {
 749:   code: GenerationErrorBody["code"];
 750:   pool_id: string | null;
 751:   decision_id: string | null;
 752:   constructor(body: GenerationErrorBody) {
 753:     super(body.message);
 754:     this.name = "GenerationBadRequestError";
 755:     this.code = body.code;
 756:     this.pool_id = body.pool_id;
 757:     this.decision_id = body.decision_id;
 758:   }
 759: }
 760: 
 761: export async function runGeneration(
 762:   pool: EvidencePool,
 763:   scope_class?: string | null,
 764: ): Promise<GenerationSuccessResponse> {
 765:   const response = await fetch(`${BACKEND_URL}/api/generation`, {
 766:     method: "POST",
 767:     headers: { "content-type": "application/json" },
 768:     body: JSON.stringify({ pool, scope_class: scope_class ?? null }),
 769:   });
 770: 
 771:   if (response.status === 400) {
 772:     const detail = await response.json().catch(() => null);
 773:     const body = (detail?.detail ?? detail) as GenerationErrorBody | null;
 774:     if (body && body.error) {
 775:       throw new GenerationBadRequestError(body);
 776:     }
 777:   }
 778: 
 779:   return asJsonOrThrow<GenerationSuccessResponse>(response);
 780: }

 785:   pipeline_stages: string[];
 786:   completion_backend: string;
 787: }

 789: export async function getGenerationHealth(): Promise<GenerationHealthResponse> {
 790:   const response = await fetch(`${BACKEND_URL}/api/generation/health`);
 791:   return asJsonOrThrow<GenerationHealthResponse>(response);
 792: }

 794: /** Filter to non-dropped sections (verified + regenerated). */
 795: export function keptSections(report: VerifiedReport): VerifiedReportSection[] {
 796:   return report.sections.filter((s) => s.section_status !== "dropped");
 797: }

 799: /** Filter sentences to those that passed strict_verify. */
 800: export function keptSentences(
 801:   section: VerifiedReportSection,
 802: ): ReportVerifiedSentence[] {
 803:   return section.verified_sentences.filter((s) => s.verifier_pass);
 804: }

 806: // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
 807: // Slice 004 — Audit bundle (GPG-signed .tar.gz export)
 808: // Mirrors src/polaris_graph/api/audit_bundle_route.py.
 809: // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
 810: 
 811: export interface AuditBundleErrorBody {
 812:   error: true;
 813:   code:
 814:     | "fk_chain_mismatch"
 815:     | "verdict_not_success"
 816:     | "cited_span_unreachable_after_snapshot"
 817:     | "copyrighted_span_in_bundle"
 818:     | "gpg_unavailable"
 819:     | "sign_failed";
 820:   message: string;
 821:   report_id: string | null;
 822: }
 823: 
 824: export class AuditBundleError extends Error {
 825:   code: AuditBundleErrorBody["code"];
 826:   report_id: string | null;
 827:   constructor(body: AuditBundleErrorBody) {
 828:     super(body.message);
 829:     this.name = "AuditBundleError";
 830:     this.code = body.code;
 831:     this.report_id = body.report_id;
 832:   }
 833: }
 834: 
 835: /**
 836:  * Build + download a GPG-signed audit bundle for the given research chain.
 837:  *
 838:  * Returns a Blob of the audit_<id>.tar.gz on success. On HTTP 4xx/5xx
 839:  * with a structured error body, throws AuditBundleError with the .code
 840:  * field set so callers can show specific UX (e.g. "GPG signer not
 841:  * configured" vs "report verdict not success").
 842:  */
 843: export async function downloadAuditBundle(
 844:   decision: IntakeScopeDecision,
 845:   pool: EvidencePool,
 846:   report: VerifiedReport,
 847: ): Promise<Blob> {
 848:   const response = await fetch(`${BACKEND_URL}/api/audit-bundle`, {
 849:     method: "POST",
 850:     headers: { "content-type": "application/json" },
 851:     body: JSON.stringify({ decision, pool, report }),
 852:   });
 853:   if (!response.ok) {
 854:     const detail = await response.json().catch(() => null);
 855:     const body = (detail?.detail ?? detail) as AuditBundleErrorBody | null;
 856:     if (body && body.error) {
 857:       throw new AuditBundleError(body);
 858:     }
 859:     throw new Error(`Audit bundle request failed: HTTP ${response.status}`);
 860:   }
 861:   return await response.blob();
 862: }
 863: 
 864: export interface BundlePreviewByContentType {
 865:   count: number;
 866:   bytes: number;
 867: }
 868: 
 869: export interface BundlePreviewResponse {
 870:   preview_bundle_id: string;
 871:   generator_model: string;
 872:   polaris_version: string;
 873:   file_count: number;
 874:   total_bytes: number;
 875:   content_type_breakdown: Record<string, BundlePreviewByContentType>;
 876: }
 877: 
 878: export async function previewAuditBundle(
 879:   decision: IntakeScopeDecision,
 880:   pool: EvidencePool,
 881:   report: VerifiedReport,
 882: ): Promise<BundlePreviewResponse> {
 883:   const response = await fetch(`${BACKEND_URL}/api/audit-bundle/preview`, {
 884:     method: "POST",
 885:     headers: { "content-type": "application/json" },
 886:     body: JSON.stringify({ decision, pool, report }),
 887:   });
 888:   if (!response.ok) {
 889:     const detail = await response.json().catch(() => null);
 890:     const body = (detail?.detail ?? detail) as AuditBundleErrorBody | null;
 891:     if (body && body.error) {
 892:       throw new AuditBundleError(body);
 893:     }
 894:     throw new Error(`Bundle preview request failed: HTTP ${response.status}`);
 895:   }
 896:   return (await response.json()) as BundlePreviewResponse;
 897: }

 902:   pipeline_stages: string[];

1033: export async function listMemory(ws: string): Promise<MemoryEntry[]> {

1037: export async function rememberMemory(
1038:   ws: string,
1039:   payload: {

1041:     content: string;
1042:     derived_from_run_ids?: string[];
1043:   },
1044: ): Promise<MemoryEntry> {

1051:   );
1052: }

1054: export async function forgetMemory(
1055:   ws: string,
1056:   entry_id: string,
1057: ): Promise<void> {
1058:   const r = await fetch(`${_ws(ws)}/${encodeURIComponent(entry_id)}`, {
1059:     method: "DELETE",
1060:   });
1061:   if (!r.ok && r.status !== 404)
1062:     throw new Error(`forget memory failed: HTTP ${r.status}`);
1063: }

1129:     ) as ApiError;
1130:     err.status = res.status;
1131:     throw err;
1132:   }
1133:   return res.json() as Promise<GraphPayload>;

  63: def _derive_slug(template_id: str, question: str) -> str:
  64:     """Deterministic URL-safe slug for pipeline-A run_dir nesting.
  65: 
  66:     Pipeline-A reads q['slug']; this produces a stable, human-readable
  67:     identifier per (template, question) pair. UUID provides uniqueness
  68:     via the artifact_dir parent — slug itself doesn't need to be unique.
  69:     """
  70:     base = f"{template_id}_{question[:60]}"
  71:     cleaned = re.sub(r"[^a-z0-9_]+", "_", base.lower()).strip("_")
  72:     return cleaned[:120] or "untitled"
  73: 
  74: 
  75: @dramatiq.actor(max_retries=ENQUEUE_MAX_RETRIES, time_limit=30 * 60 * 1000)
  76: def enqueue_research_run(run_id: str, request_payload: dict[str, Any]) -> dict[str, Any]:
  77:     """Execute a research run via pipeline-A. Idempotent on run_id.
  78: 
  79:     Stub-mode path: when run_store has no row for run_id (tests use
  80:     .fn() directly without insert_run), returns deterministic noop
  81:     without DB writes — preserves I-phase0-005 stub-mode semantics.
  82: 
  83:     Production path: marks in_progress, builds q-dict with v6 fields,
  84:     invokes scripts.run_honest_sweep_r3.run_one_query, parses the
  85:     manifest.json pipeline-A writes, and dispatches to
  86:     mark_completed / mark_aborted / mark_failed per pipeline_status.
  87:     """
  88:     # Stub-mode preservation
  89:     if run_store.get_run(run_id) is None:
  90:         return {"run_id": run_id, "status": "completed", "echo": request_payload}
  91: 
  92:     run_store.mark_in_progress(run_id)
  93:     decision_id = str(uuid.uuid4())
  94:     output_root = Path(os.environ.get("POLARIS_V6_OUTPUT_ROOT", "outputs/v6_runs"))
  95:     artifact_dir_root = output_root / run_id
  96:     artifact_dir_root.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
  97: 
  98:     template_id = request_payload.get("template", "")
  99:     question = request_payload.get("question", "")
 100:     domain = TEMPLATE_TO_SCOPE_DOMAIN.get(template_id, "policy")
 101:     slug = _derive_slug(template_id, question)
 102: 
 103:     # v6 fields flow through q-dict (NO os.environ mutation per iter-2 P1.2)
 104:     q: dict[str, Any] = {
 105:         "domain": domain,
 106:         "slug": slug,
 107:         "question": question,
 108:         "external_run_id": run_id,
 109:         "decision_id": decision_id,
 110:         "v6_mode": True,
 111:         "out_root_override": str(artifact_dir_root),
 112:         "template_id": template_id,
 113:     }
 114: 
 115:     # I-arch-001b: synthesize v30.1 contract patch from v6 template's
 116:     # frame_manifest. Pipeline-A merges this into the scope template's
 117:     # per_query_report_contract before compile_frame / load_report_contract_for_slug.
 118:     # Failure is graceful (logger.warning) — pipeline-A handles missing
 119:     # contract via legacy no-contract path.
 120:     try:
 121:         from polaris_v6.templates.registry import load_template
 122:         from src.polaris_graph.v30_contract_synthesizer import build_v30_contract
 123: 
 124:         v6_tmpl = load_template(template_id).model_dump()
 125:         q["v30_contract_patch"] = build_v30_contract(v6_tmpl, slug, question)
 126:         logger.info(
 127:             "[actor] v30_contract_patch synthesized run_id=%s template_id=%s slug=%s entities=%d",
 128:             run_id,
 129:             template_id,
 130:             slug,
 131:             len(q["v30_contract_patch"][slug]["required_entities"]),
 132:         )
 133:     except FileNotFoundError as exc:
 134:         logger.warning(
 135:             "[actor] v6 template not found template_id=%s run_id=%s; "
 136:             "pipeline-A will run on legacy no-contract path: %s",
 137:             template_id,
 138:             run_id,
 139:             exc,
 140:         )
 141:     except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001 — synthesizer failure must not block runtime
 142:         logger.warning(
 143:             "[actor] v30_contract_patch synthesis FAILED run_id=%s template_id=%s "
 144:             "slug=%s: %s; pipeline-A on legacy no-contract path",
 145:             run_id,
 146:             template_id,
 147:             slug,
 148:             exc,
 149:             exc_info=True,
 150:         )
 151: 
 152:     run_store.set_pipeline_meta(
 153:         run_id,
 154:         query_slug=slug,
 155:         artifact_dir=str(artifact_dir_root),
 156:         decision_id=decision_id,
 157:     )
 158: 
 159:     try:
 160:         from scripts.run_honest_sweep_r3 import run_one_query
 161: 
 162:         summary = asyncio.run(run_one_query(q, artifact_dir_root))
 163:     except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001 — actor must record any pipeline crash
 164:         logger.exception("[actor] pipeline-A raised for run_id=%s", run_id)
 165:         run_store.mark_failed(run_id, f"pipeline_exception: {type(exc).__name__}: {exc}")
 166:         raise
 167: 
 168:     # Parse pipeline-A's manifest.json — written at every exit path
 169:     manifest_path = artifact_dir_root / "manifest.json"
 170:     if not manifest_path.is_file():
 171:         run_store.mark_failed(
 172:             run_id, "manifest_missing: pipeline-A returned without writing manifest.json"
 173:         )
 174:         return summary
 175:     try:
 176:         manifest = json.loads(manifest_path.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
 177:     except json.JSONDecodeError as exc:
 178:         run_store.mark_failed(run_id, f"manifest_invalid: {exc}")
 179:         return summary
 180: 
 181:     pipeline_status = manifest.get("status") or "error_unexpected"
 182:     manifest_run_id = manifest.get("run_id")
 183:     cost_usd = manifest.get("cost_usd")
 184:     if cost_usd is None:
 185:         cost_usd = summary.get("cost_usd")
 186:     try:
 187:         cost_usd_f = float(cost_usd) if cost_usd is not None else None
 188:     except (TypeError, ValueError):
 189:         cost_usd_f = None
 190: 
 191:     run_store.set_pipeline_meta(run_id, manifest_run_id=manifest_run_id)
 192: 
 193:     if pipeline_status == "success" or pipeline_status.startswith("partial_"):
 194:         run_store.mark_completed(
 195:             run_id, summary, pipeline_status=pipeline_status, cost_usd=cost_usd_f
 196:         )
 197:     elif pipeline_status.startswith("abort_"):
 198:         run_store.mark_aborted(
 199:             run_id,
 200:             pipeline_status=pipeline_status,
 201:             abort_reason=manifest.get("error") or pipeline_status,
 202:             cost_usd=cost_usd_f,
 203:         )
 204:     elif pipeline_status.startswith("error_"):
 205:         run_store.mark_failed(
 206:             run_id, f"pipeline_error: {pipeline_status}: {manifest.get('error', '')}"
 207:         )
 208:     else:
 209:         run_store.mark_failed(run_id, f"unknown_pipeline_status: {pipeline_status!r}")
 210:     return summary

 213: @dramatiq.actor(max_retries=0)

   1: """Runs router — POST /runs, GET /runs/{id}.
   2: 
   3: I-phase0-005 (Codex APPROVE iter 4): persist runs to SQLite via

   5: Replaces the previous in-memory `_run_table` dict.

  39:         raise HTTPException(status_code=500, detail="run row vanished after insert")

  46:     if record is None:
  47:         raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail=f"run {run_id!r} not found")
  48:     return record

 succeeded in 3593ms:
   1: """In-memory workspace memory store (Phase 2B substrate).
   2: 
   3: Phase 2B production swap: replace `_recall_by_keyword` with Chroma
   4: semantic recall once the v6 cluster is live. The interface stays
   5: stable so callers don't change.
   6: 
   7: Per CLAUDE.md security posture: workspace_id MUST be normalized
   8: identically on write and read; mismatch is a P0 governance issue
   9: (per `.codex/REVIEW_BRIEF_FORMAT.md` example).
  10: """
  11: 
  12: from __future__ import annotations
  13: 
  14: import math
  15: import re
  16: import uuid
  17: from collections import Counter
  18: from datetime import datetime, timezone
  19: 
  20: from polaris_v6.memory.schema import (
  21:     MemoryEntry,
  22:     MemoryKind,
  23:     MemoryQuery,
  24:     MemoryRecallResult,
  25: )
  26: 
  27: 
  28: def _normalize_workspace_id(raw: str) -> str:
  29:     return raw.strip().lower()
  30: 
  31: 
  32: def _now_iso() -> str:
  33:     return datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat()
  34: 
  35: 
  36: _TOKEN_RE = re.compile(r"[a-z0-9]+")
  37: 
  38: 
  39: def _tokens(text: str) -> Counter[str]:
  40:     return Counter(_TOKEN_RE.findall(text.lower()))
  41: 
  42: 
  43: def _cosine(a: Counter[str], b: Counter[str]) -> float:
  44:     if not a or not b:
  45:         return 0.0
  46:     dot = sum(a[k] * b[k] for k in a.keys() & b.keys())
  47:     norm_a = math.sqrt(sum(v * v for v in a.values()))
  48:     norm_b = math.sqrt(sum(v * v for v in b.values()))
  49:     if norm_a == 0 or norm_b == 0:
  50:         return 0.0
  51:     return dot / (norm_a * norm_b)
  52: 
  53: 
  54: class WorkspaceMemoryStore:
  55:     """In-memory implementation. Production swaps for Chroma in Phase 2B."""
  56: 
  57:     def __init__(self) -> None:
  58:         self._entries: dict[str, MemoryEntry] = {}
  59: 
  60:     def remember(
  61:         self,
  62:         *,
  63:         workspace_id: str,
  64:         kind: MemoryKind,
  65:         content: str,
  66:         derived_from_run_ids: list[str] | None = None,
  67:     ) -> MemoryEntry:
  68:         entry = MemoryEntry(
  69:             entry_id=uuid.uuid4().hex,
  70:             workspace_id=_normalize_workspace_id(workspace_id),
  71:             kind=kind,
  72:             content=content,
  73:             created_at=_now_iso(),
  74:             derived_from_run_ids=derived_from_run_ids or [],
  75:         )
  76:         self._entries[entry.entry_id] = entry
  77:         return entry
  78: 
  79:     def recall(self, query: MemoryQuery) -> list[MemoryRecallResult]:
  80:         ws_norm = _normalize_workspace_id(query.workspace_id)
  81:         candidates = [
  82:             e
  83:             for e in self._entries.values()
  84:             if e.workspace_id == ws_norm
  85:             and (query.kinds is None or e.kind in query.kinds)
  86:         ]
  87:         if not candidates:
  88:             return []
  89:         q_tokens = _tokens(query.query_text)
  90:         scored = [
  91:             MemoryRecallResult(entry=e, score=_cosine(q_tokens, _tokens(e.content)))
  92:             for e in candidates
  93:         ]
  94:         scored.sort(key=lambda r: r.score, reverse=True)
  95:         for r in scored[: query.top_k]:
  96:             r.entry.use_count += 1
  97:             r.entry.last_used_at = _now_iso()
  98:         return scored[: query.top_k]
  99: 
 100:     def forget(self, *, workspace_id: str, entry_id: str) -> bool:
 101:         entry = self._entries.get(entry_id)
 102:         if entry is None:
 103:             return False
 104:         if entry.workspace_id != _normalize_workspace_id(workspace_id):
 105:             return False
 106:         del self._entries[entry_id]
 107:         return True
 108: 
 109:     def list_workspace(self, workspace_id: str) -> list[MemoryEntry]:
 110:         ws_norm = _normalize_workspace_id(workspace_id)
 111:         return [e for e in self._entries.values() if e.workspace_id == ws_norm]

 succeeded in 3990ms:
   1: """ChromaDB-backed semantic workspace memory store (I-f14-001).
   2: 
   3: Cosine vector recall. Production sentence-transformers + router swap
   4: deferred to I-f14-001b. Per CLAUDE.md §8.4 tests inject embed_fn.
   5: """
   6: 
   7: from __future__ import annotations
   8: 
   9: import os
  10: import uuid
  11: from collections.abc import Callable
  12: from datetime import datetime, timezone
  13: 
  14: from polaris_v6.memory.schema import (
  15:     MemoryEntry,
  16:     MemoryKind,
  17:     MemoryQuery,
  18:     MemoryRecallResult,
  19: )
  20: 
  21: EmbedFn = Callable[[list[str]], list[list[float]]]
  22: 
  23: 
  24: def _default_embed_fn(_t: list[str]) -> list[list[float]]:
  25:     raise RuntimeError("inject embed_fn (sentence-transformers); deferred to I-f14-001b")
  26: 
  27: 
  28: def _norm(raw: str) -> str:
  29:     return raw.strip().lower()
  30: 
  31: 
  32: def _now() -> str:
  33:     return datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat()
  34: 
  35: 
  36: def _meta(e: MemoryEntry) -> dict:
  37:     return {"workspace_id": e.workspace_id, "kind": e.kind, "entry_json": e.model_dump_json()}
  38: 
  39: 
  40: class ChromaWorkspaceMemoryStore:
  41:     def __init__(self, *, persist_directory: str | None, embed_fn: EmbedFn | None = None,
  42:                  collection_name: str = "v6_workspace_memory") -> None:
  43:         import chromadb
  44:         from chromadb.config import Settings
  45:         s = Settings(anonymized_telemetry=False)
  46:         if persist_directory is not None:
  47:             os.makedirs(persist_directory, exist_ok=True)
  48:             client = chromadb.PersistentClient(path=persist_directory, settings=s)
  49:         else:
  50:             client = chromadb.EphemeralClient(settings=s)
  51:         self._collection = client.get_or_create_collection(
  52:             name=collection_name, metadata={"hnsw:space": "cosine"})
  53:         space = (self._collection.metadata or {}).get("hnsw:space")
  54:         if space != "cosine":
  55:             raise RuntimeError(
  56:                 f"collection {collection_name} pre-existing with hnsw:space={space!r}; expected cosine")
  57:         self._embed_fn: EmbedFn = embed_fn or _default_embed_fn
  58: 
  59:     def remember(self, *, workspace_id: str, kind: MemoryKind, content: str,
  60:                  derived_from_run_ids: list[str] | None = None) -> MemoryEntry:
  61:         emb = self._embed_fn([content])[0]
  62:         entry = MemoryEntry(
  63:             entry_id=uuid.uuid4().hex, workspace_id=_norm(workspace_id), kind=kind,
  64:             content=content, embedding_vector=emb, created_at=_now(),
  65:             derived_from_run_ids=derived_from_run_ids or [])
  66:         self._collection.add(
  67:             ids=[entry.entry_id], documents=[content], embeddings=[emb], metadatas=[_meta(entry)])
  68:         return entry
  69: 
  70:     def recall(self, query: MemoryQuery) -> list[MemoryRecallResult]:
  71:         if query.kinds is not None and len(query.kinds) == 0:
  72:             return []
  73:         ws = _norm(query.workspace_id)
  74:         where: dict = {"workspace_id": ws} if query.kinds is None else {
  75:             "$and": [{"workspace_id": ws}, {"kind": {"$in": list(query.kinds)}}]}
  76:         emb = self._embed_fn([query.query_text])[0]
  77:         result = self._collection.query(query_embeddings=[emb], n_results=query.top_k, where=where)
  78:         ids = (result.get("ids") or [[]])[0]
  79:         distances = (result.get("distances") or [[]])[0]
  80:         metas = (result.get("metadatas") or [[]])[0]
  81:         out: list[MemoryRecallResult] = []
  82:         now = _now()
  83:         for entry_id, distance, meta in zip(ids, distances, metas):
  84:             entry = MemoryEntry.model_validate_json(meta["entry_json"])
  85:             entry.use_count += 1
  86:             entry.last_used_at = now
  87:             self._collection.update(ids=[entry_id], metadatas=[_meta(entry)])
  88:             out.append(MemoryRecallResult(entry=entry, score=max(0.0, min(1.0, 1.0 - float(distance)))))
  89:         return out
  90: 
  91:     def forget(self, *, workspace_id: str, entry_id: str) -> bool:
  92:         existing = self._collection.get(ids=[entry_id], include=["metadatas"])
  93:         metas = existing.get("metadatas") or []
  94:         if not metas or metas[0].get("workspace_id") != _norm(workspace_id):
  95:             return False
  96:         self._collection.delete(ids=[entry_id])
  97:         return True
  98: 
  99:     def list_workspace(self, workspace_id: str) -> list[MemoryEntry]:
 100:         result = self._collection.get(where={"workspace_id": _norm(workspace_id)}, include=["metadatas"])
 101:         return [MemoryEntry.model_validate_json(m["entry_json"]) for m in (result.get("metadatas") or [])]

 succeeded in 4503ms:
   1: """F14 workspace memory HTTP endpoints (Phase 2B Task 2B.6).

   5: """
   6: 
   7: from __future__ import annotations
   8: 
   9: from fastapi import APIRouter, HTTPException
  10: from pydantic import BaseModel, Field
  11: 
  12: from polaris_v6.memory.schema import (
  13:     MemoryEntry,
  14:     MemoryKind,
  15:     MemoryQuery,
  16:     MemoryRecallResult,
  17: )
  18: from polaris_v6.memory.store import WorkspaceMemoryStore
  19: 
  20: router = APIRouter(prefix="/workspaces", tags=["memory"])
  21: 
  22: _store = WorkspaceMemoryStore()
  23: 
  24: 
  25: class RememberRequest(BaseModel):
  26:     kind: MemoryKind
  27:     content: str = Field(..., min_length=4, max_length=4000)
  28:     derived_from_run_ids: list[str] = Field(default_factory=list)
  29: 
  30: 
  31: @router.post(
  32:     "/{workspace_id}/memory", response_model=MemoryEntry, status_code=201
  33: )
  34: def remember(workspace_id: str, payload: RememberRequest) -> MemoryEntry:
  35:     return _store.remember(
  36:         workspace_id=workspace_id,
  37:         kind=payload.kind,
  38:         content=payload.content,
  39:         derived_from_run_ids=payload.derived_from_run_ids,
  40:     )
  41: 
  42: 
  43: class RecallRequest(BaseModel):
  44:     query_text: str = Field(..., min_length=1, max_length=2000)
  45:     kinds: list[MemoryKind] | None = None
  46:     top_k: int = Field(default=5, ge=1, le=50)
  47: 
  48: 
  49: @router.post(
  50:     "/{workspace_id}/memory/recall",
  51:     response_model=list[MemoryRecallResult],
  52: )
  53: def recall(workspace_id: str, payload: RecallRequest) -> list[MemoryRecallResult]:
  54:     return _store.recall(
  55:         MemoryQuery(
  56:             workspace_id=workspace_id,
  57:             query_text=payload.query_text,
  58:             kinds=payload.kinds,
  59:             top_k=payload.top_k,
  60:         )
  61:     )
  62: 
  63: 
  64: @router.delete("/{workspace_id}/memory/{entry_id}", status_code=204)
  65: def forget(workspace_id: str, entry_id: str) -> None:
  66:     if not _store.forget(workspace_id=workspace_id, entry_id=entry_id):
  67:         raise HTTPException(
  68:             status_code=404,
  69:             detail=f"entry {entry_id!r} not found in workspace {workspace_id!r}",
  70:         )
  71:     return None
  72: 
  73: 
  74: @router.get("/{workspace_id}/memory", response_model=list[MemoryEntry])
  75: def list_workspace(workspace_id: str) -> list[MemoryEntry]:
  76:     return _store.list_workspace(workspace_id)

docs\hardware_decision.md:1:# Hardware Decision — DeepSeek V4 Pro Sovereign Path
docs\hardware_decision.md:4:**Path selected:** **Path C — DeepSeek V4 Flash on 8× H200 OVH BHS Canada**
docs\hardware_decision.md:15:**Single sentence:** POLARIS sovereign cognition runs DeepSeek V4 Flash on **8× NVIDIA H200 GPUs** in **OVH Canada BHS region** (Path C of the three Phase 0.6 path options).
docs\hardware_decision.md:17:This decision was canonically reconciled in `docs/blockers.md §3` on 2026-05-02 via user-signed reconciliation per Plan v13 §F best-of-best lock semantics. **No silent fallback** is permitted: if Phase 0.7 SGLang/vLLM bakeoff data later contradicts the lock (e.g. V4 Flash quality fails on 2+ template families), the orchestrator halts per Plan v13 §H halt-condition #5 and the user re-signs canonical to switch paths — not a runtime auto-degradation.
docs\hardware_decision.md:25:| **A** | 16× H200 FP8 | V4 Pro full | 5+ | NOT selected |
docs\hardware_decision.md:26:| **B** | 8× H200 reduced-context | V4 Pro reduced | 2 | NOT selected |
docs\hardware_decision.md:27:| **C** | 8× H200 (procurement engaged at Phase 4 entry, NOT now) | **V4 Flash only** | 5+ (target) | **SELECTED** |
docs\hardware_decision.md:29:### 2.1 Path A (16× H200 FP8 V4 Pro full) — NOT SELECTED
docs\hardware_decision.md:31:- **Pros:** Highest model fidelity. Full V4 Pro capability surface.
docs\hardware_decision.md:32:- **Cons:** 2× hardware cost vs Path C. Procurement risk (16× H200 capacity in OVH BHS may not be available within Phase 4 timeline). No measured advantage over V4 Flash on the 8 Carney templates per substrate-audit baseline.
docs\hardware_decision.md:35:### 2.2 Path B (8× H200 reduced V4 Pro) — NOT SELECTED
docs\hardware_decision.md:37:- **Pros:** Same hardware footprint as Path C. Marginally higher per-call quality on long-context tasks vs V4 Flash.
docs\hardware_decision.md:41:### 2.3 Path C (8× H200 V4 Flash only) — SELECTED
docs\hardware_decision.md:43:- **Pros:** 5+ concurrent sessions (target capacity, to be verified at Phase 4.4 benchmark). Procurement footprint smaller than Path A (lower availability risk; OVH H200 page status `Coming soon` per `docs/blockers.md §9` — actual reservation engagement scheduled for Phase 4 entry, NOT this commit). Lowest cost. Quality differential vs V4 Pro acceptable for Carney scope per substrate audit `docs/substrate_audit_2026-05-01.md`.
docs\hardware_decision.md:44:- **Cons:** Marginal quality reduction on long-form synthesis tasks vs V4 Pro. Capability gap acknowledged below.
docs\hardware_decision.md:45:- **Selection rationale:** capacity > marginal quality for sovereign deployment at Carney scope. V4 Flash quality delta is acceptable for the use case.
docs\hardware_decision.md:49:## 3. Capability gap acknowledged (V4 Flash vs V4 Pro)
docs\hardware_decision.md:53:- **Long-form synthesis (>200-sentence reports):** V4 Pro produces marginally better discourse coherence on long syntheses. V4 Flash is acceptable but the gap is non-zero and is documented here, not silently tolerated.
docs\hardware_decision.md:54:- **Multi-jurisdictional comparison (F12, F14):** V4 Pro's larger context window can hold full bibliographic state for cross-jurisdiction comparison; V4 Flash relies more on retrieval pruning. Mitigation: F12/F14 retrieval pipeline already prunes by relevance; quality impact monitored at Phase 3.5 benchmark.
docs\hardware_decision.md:55:- **Anti-sycophancy edge cases (paired-prompt fixtures):** V4 Pro may resist sycophantic drift on adversarial paired prompts more reliably than V4 Flash. Mitigation: sycophancy CI (task 1.7) runs on every substrate change; if delta exceeds the ELEPHANT methodology bar (<10% rate, <5% paired-stance delta), Path A/B re-evaluation triggers per Plan v13 §H halt-condition #5.
docs\hardware_decision.md:66:- **Phases 0–3 (May 1 – Aug 9):** POLARIS validates via API service (OpenRouter / DeepSeek API). Build + benchmark vs ChatGPT 5.5 Pro DR + Gemini 3.1 Pro DR runs against API-provided LLM endpoints. **No bare-metal cluster commitment during build.** Per `docs/blockers.md §3` API-first sequencing.
docs\hardware_decision.md:88:- ✅ "If Path C (V4 Flash only): rationale documented + capability gap acknowledged" — §2.3 + §3 above.
docs\hardware_decision.md:101:If, during Phases 1–3, evidence accumulates that V4 Flash quality is materially insufficient for the Carney scope (e.g. >2 template families fail Phase 3.5 benchmark vs ChatGPT/Gemini DR with delta attributable to model choice rather than retrieval/synthesis), the orchestrator halts. Resolution requires user-signed canonical reconciliation switching to Path A or B. The orchestrator will NOT auto-degrade or auto-substitute.

  13: pin. §3 feature statuses are **PROVISIONAL** pending Phase 1 verification
  14: (I-rdy-002, #498); the full document is re-pinned in Phase 0B (I-rdy-003,
  15: #499) once statuses are verified.
  16: 
  17: ---
  18: 
  19: ## §1. Locked constraints — frozen, do not reopen
  20: 
  21: ### §1.1 LLM
  22: - **Generator: DeepSeek V4 Pro** — 1.6-trillion-parameter MoE (49B active/token), ships FP4 (experts) + FP8 (rest). Operator-locked, stated 6+ times.
  23: - **Evaluator: Gemma 4 31B.**
  24: - **Two-family invariant:** generator (DeepSeek lineage) and evaluator (Google/Gemma lineage) are different training lineages. `openrouter_client.check_family_segregation` enforces this at construction.
  25: - **Concurrency: 1 concurrent session.** Traffic is operator-limited. This does not shrink the GPU requirement (the 1.6T model needs ~880 GB VRAM regardless of concurrency); it only bounds KV-cache headroom.
  26: - Supersedes the prior `docs/hardware_decision.md` "Path C / V4 Flash" — that was overridden by operator directive to V4 Pro.
  27: 
  28: ### §1.2 Hardware / sovereignty
  29: - **Canadian sovereign GPU only.** V4 Pro requires 8× H200 or 12-16× H100 (Hopper-class — V100/A100 lack FP4/FP8 and cannot run it).
  30: - **Not** OVH Canada — its Canadian datacentre (Beauharnois, Québec) has only old V100/V100S GPUs, which lack FP4/FP8 and cannot run V4 Pro; OVH's Hopper-class H100/H200/A100 are France-only (verified via the OVH API; see `state/canada_gpu_research_2026_05_15.md`). **Not** France. **Not** any US company.
  31: - Canadian sovereign GPU candidates: Vexxhost (Montréal), ISAIC (Edmonton) — both pending physical-location confirmation.
  32: - Sovereignty threat model is NARROW: no runtime US LLM vendor calls + no report data in US jurisdiction. Model lineage, build toolchain, and DNS registrar are out of scope of the threat model.
  33: - The orchestrator (CPU box) stays on OVH BHS5 — that is Canadian and acceptable; only the LLM inference must move to a Canadian GPU.
  34: 
  35: ### §1.3 Architecture
  36: - **The deployed v6 stack** is the architecture: `redis + api (FastAPI v6.2.0) + worker (Dramatiq) + webui (Next.js 16)` via `docker-compose.v6.yml`. **No rewrites.**
  37: - Pipeline B (legacy LangGraph) and Pipeline C (frozen CLI) are **out of demo scope.**
  38: - Frontend stack: Next.js 16 + React 19 + shadcn/ui + Tailwind 4.
  39: 
  40: ### §1.4 Naming
  41: - The legacy name **"BPEI" is banned** in all demo-facing code, docs, and config. Renamed to `ambiguity_detector`. Rename tracked by #434 (I-naming-001) + readiness Phase 2.
  42: 
  43: ### §1.5 Demo
  44: - Single venue, PM Mark Carney's office, flexible date in June 2026.
  45: - Scope = the Carney plan (`docs/carney_delivery_plan_v6_2.md`). **No features beyond it.**
  46: 
  47: ---
  48: 
  49: ## §2. Canonical templates — the 8, frozen
  50: 
  51: The single source of truth is `config/scope_templates/`. Frontend cards,
  52: frontend TypeScript types, and the v6 backend registry MUST all read from it
  53: (reconciliation tracked by I-rdy-005, #501).
  54: 
  55: 1. `clinical` — clinical drug audit
  56: 2. `policy` — public policy
  57: 3. `tech` — technology assessment
  58: 4. `due_diligence` — due diligence
  59: 5. `ai_sovereignty` — AI sovereignty
  60: 6. `canada_us` — Canada–US
  61: 7. `workforce` — workforce
  62: 8. `custom` — operator-defined catch-all
  63: 
  64: No template is added or removed without an operator-acknowledged change to §2 (per the §5 change protocol).
  65:

  71: (`state/carney_readiness_gaps_2026_05_15.md`), to be verified by Phase 1
  72: (I-rdy-002, #498) and re-pinned in Phase 0B (I-rdy-003, #499).
  73: 
  74: **Binding rule:** *harness-page evidence and golden-fixture tests do NOT count
  75: as feature-complete.* A feature is "complete" only when a live run exercises
  76: it end-to-end in the deployed product.

 122: ## §5. Change protocol
 123: 
 124: - §1 (constraints) changes ONLY via an operator-signed reconciliation commit that explicitly states the prior value, the new value, and the reason.
 125: - §2 / §3 / §4 changes require an operator-acknowledged commit.
 126: - A Codex consult, advisor, or Claude reasoning step may *recommend* a change but may NOT enact one — §1 is operator-owned.
 127: - This is the structural defense against the swing pattern (LLM drifting V4 Pro→Flash→Llama; provider drifting Canada→France) seen 2026-05-15.

.codex/I-rdy-002\codex_verdict.txt:1924:state\codex_consultations\plan_v9_codex_response.md:508:P1 - §C-rerun still exposes `OPENAI_API_KEY` to the untrusted Codex process. Pattern scanning is a tripwire, not a complete exfiltration control. .codex/I-rdy-002\codex_verdict.txt:1935:state\codex_consultations\bootstrap_round2_response.md:1195:.\outputs\audits\continuous\4f20140_audit.md:29:- **C-sec-tab-semantics-auth-leak (probe 8):** PASS. For unauthenticated/invalid-runId paths, `bundle` is `null` and only the error banner (`{error && ...}` at `:118-130`) renders.
